disease.name: Cystitis (Bladder Inflammation)
disease.note: Inflammation of bladder wall. Most commonly infectious (bacterial cystitis). Non-infectious causes: interstitial cystitis, radiation cystitis, drug-induced (cyclophosphamide).

severityAssessment.severityLabel: Uncomplicated (mild, responsive) vs Complicated (hemorrhagic, recurrent, non-infectious).

subtypes.name: Infectious (Bacterial) Cystitis
subtypes.management: Antibiotics: nitrofurantoin 100 mg BID x 5 days, TMP-SMX DS BID x 3 days, or fosfomycin 3g single dose. Pain relief. Increase fluids. Phenazopyridine for dysuria.

subtypes.name: Interstitial Cystitis / Bladder Pain Syndrome
subtypes.management: Multimodal: dietary modification (avoid acidic foods, caffeine, alcohol). Oral: amitriptyline, hydroxyzine, pentosan polysulfate (Elmiron). Bladder instillations (DMSO, heparin, lidocaine). Physical therapy for pelvic floor. Hydrodistension under anesthesia. Neuromodulation.

subtypes.name: Radiation Cystitis
subtypes.management: Mild: fluid hydration, avoidance of bladder irritants. For hematuria: oral tranexamic acid. Persistent: alum irrigation, formalin instillation, hyperbaric oxygen therapy. Cystoscopy with fulguration of bleeding points. Severe: intravesical formalin, embolization, surgical diversion.

subtypes.name: Hemorrhagic Cystitis (Chemotherapy-induced)
subtypes.management: Prevention: hydration, mesna with cyclophosphamide. Treatment: aggressive hydration, continuous bladder irrigation (NS at 300 mL/h). Transfusion if needed. Cystoscopy with clot evacuation and fulguration. Intravesical alum or formalin. Severe: embolization, cystectomy.

laboratory.name: Urine cytology
laboratory.indication: if persistent hematuria, rule out malignancy

imaging.name: Cystoscopy
imaging.indication: suspected interstitial cystitis, radiation cystitis, or tumor

management.protocolReference: EAU Guidelines on Urological Infections / AUA/SUFU Interstitial Cystitis

pathways.criteria: acute onset, positive dipstick, no complications
pathways.treatment: Short-course antibiotics (3-5 days depending on agent). Nitrofurantoin first-line. Analgesia. Follow-up culture if symptoms persist.

pathways.criteria: chronic pain, negative cultures, symptom <12 months
pathways.treatment: Diet modification. Oral amitriptyline 10-50 mg. Bladder instillations (DMSO cocktail). Pelvic PT. Annual cystoscopy.

Continuous bladder irrigation (NS at 300 mL/h IV). IV fluids. Transfusion PRN. Cystoscopic clot evacuation + fulguration. Intravesical alum 1% or formalin 4%. Hyperbaric oxygen. Embolization/surgery if refractory.
